intro: The lower limb is built for weight bearing and movement. It contains the strongest bones in the body.
notes: Femur: The longest and strongest bone in the body.
Patella: The kneecap that protects the knee joint.
Tibia: The larger 'shin bone' of the lower leg.
Tarsals: Bones that make up the ankle.

hotspots.label: Femur
hotspots.description:
The Femur is the thigh bone. It is the longest, heaviest, and strongest bone in the body.

The top head fits into the Acetabulum (socket) of the pelvic girdle.
hotspots.examTip: Why is it so big? Because it has to support the weight of the entire upper body.

hotspots.label: Patella
hotspots.description:
The Patella (Kneecap) is a triangular bone located in front of the knee joint.

It is a 'Sesamoid' bone, meaning it is embedded within a tendon.
hotspots.examTip: Its function is to prevent the knee from bending forward (hyper-extension).

hotspots.label: Tibia
hotspots.description:
The Tibia is the larger, inner bone of the lower leg. It is often called the 'Shin Bone'.

It connects the knee to the ankle and bears most of the body's weight.
hotspots.examTip: How to identify: The Tibia is THICK (like a tube) and is on the inner side (Big Toe side).

hotspots.label: Fibula
hotspots.description:
The Fibula is the thin bone located on the outer side of the lower leg.

Unlike the Tibia, it does not bear weight but provides a place for muscles to attach.
hotspots.examTip: How to identify: The Fibula is FINE (thin) like a flute.

hotspots.label: Tarsals & Feet
hotspots.description:
Tarsals: The 7 bones of the ankle (including the heel).
Metatarsals: The 5 bones of the foot.
Phalanges: The bones of the toes.
hotspots.examTip: The heel bone is called the Calcaneus.
